location.office_time_required: remote eligible; in-person onboarding required if hired

level_signals: 8+ years in Infrastructure TPM or related roles
Expected to engage executive leadership and external partners independently
Base range $185K–$317K USD, consistent with senior/staff IC
Expected to set up and scale TPM functions

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years in Infrastructure TPM or related roles (cloud engineering, SRE, or infra-focused software engineering), with hands-on experience in cloud-native architectures and distributed systems (AWS, GCP, or similar)

Deep technical expertise across infrastructure components - storage (S3, RDS, DynamoDB), caching (Redis), search (OpenSearch), and event-driven systems (Kafka) - with the ability to reason across org-level goals, architectural trade-offs, and component-level details

Track record driving large-scale infrastructure programs such as migrations, cost optimization, encryption/key management, and reliability initiatives for high-availability, business-critical services

Strong cross-functional program management skills, including coordinating multi-team engineering orgs, tracking performance bottlenecks, optimizing infrastructure SLAs, and establishing PM best practices in ambiguous environments

Proven stakeholder management, with experience engaging external partners (cloud providers, enterprise customers) and translating complex technical challenges into clear executive-level communications

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: A track record of setting up and scaling TPM functions or program management practices within an engineering organization
Experience working on high-visibility enterprise security and compliance programs (SOC 2, FedRAMP, encryption key management)
Infrastructure incident management and analysis experience
Experience working with external enterprise partners on technical programs (e.g., Apple, AWS, Google)
Background in software engineering or site reliability engineering (SWE/SRE-to-TPM career path)

scope.surface: Infrastructure program management — reliability, storage, distributed systems, cloud-native performance, compliance (FedRAMP, SOC 2, encryption key management)
cross_functional_partners: External enterprise partners (cloud providers, enterprise customers)
core_responsibilities: Lead the execution, coordination, and risk management of Figma's infrastructure projects, ensuring seamless integration with minimal performance impact

Drive key infrastructure initiatives, including reliability, storage, distributed systems, cloud-native performance improvements, and compliance programs (e.g., encryption key management, FedRAMP, SOC 2)
Partner closely with engineering, security, compliance, and legal teams to ensure alignment and on-time delivery
Track program milestones and ensure seamless delivery across multiple infrastructure teams, including data, caching, observability, and security engineering
Provide regular updates to executive leadership, external partners, and internal teams on the status of infrastructure programs, including risks, blockers, and dependencies
Develop and drive best practices in infrastructure program management, improving visibility into progress, risks, and technical dependencies
Facilitate large-scale testing and rollout strategies for critical infrastructure changes to minimize downtime and ensure high system reliability
Translate technical constraints and risks into executive-level communications for senior leadership and external stakeholders

green_flags: Wide base salary range disclosed ($185K–$317K) indicating transparency
Fully remote-US eligible, not restricted to hub offices
Explicitly encourages candidates to apply even if experience doesn't align perfectly
Clear scope and ownership area — infrastructure TPM greenspace
Equity and robust benefits package mentioned explicitly
Real technical depth expected (specific infra tools named), signaling a genuine engineering-focused TPM role
red_flags: Very wide comp range ($185K–$317K) makes it difficult to calibrate level expectations
Remote pay is localized at 80–100% of stated range, so actual remote compensation may be substantially lower than stated max
8+ years requirement is hard; no stated flexibility for fewer years with exceptional credentials
In-person onboarding is required despite remote-eligible status — logistics consideration for some candidates
Visa sponsorship status not addressed anywhere in the JD

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and 8+ years requirement)
scope.business_model (inferred from Figma's publicly known product)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly stated)
ambiguities: Pay range ($185K–$317K) is extremely wide; JD notes it applies to SF and NY hub offices, with remote roles localized at 80–100% — actual salary for remote workers could be significantly lower than the stated maximum
The JD lists the role as available from 'US hubs or remotely in the United States' — treated as remote-us eligible with hybrid optional
Visa sponsorship is not mentioned at all; marked as unclear rather than assumed open
